title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Chicken: Authentic Korean Fried Chicken Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Yangnyeom Chicken with our easy recipe! Get crispy Korean fried chicken coated in a sweet & spicy sauce. Perfect for dinner tonight!
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade sweet and spicy Korean fried chicken
crispy Korean fried chicken sauce recipe
Korean fried chicken with gochujang sauce
seo.h1: Authentic Yangnyeom Chicken Recipe: Korean Fried Chicken
h2Headings: What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy Yangnyeom Chicken, Korean fried chicken coated in a glossy sweet and spicy red sauce, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the irresistible crunch and flavor of homemade Yangnyeom Chicken, the ultimate Korean fried chicken experience!

description: Yangnyeom Chicken is the iconic Korean fried chicken coated in a luscious, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. Each bite offers an irresistible crunch followed by a burst of savory, tangy, and subtly fiery flavors. This dish is a true celebration of Korean comfort food, perfect for sharing with friends and family.

ingredients.item: Bone-in, skin-on chicken wings and drumettes
ingredients.notes: or boneless chicken thighs, cut into 1.5-inch pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season them all over with salt, black pepper, and garlic powder. In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and baking powder. Gradually add cold water to the flour mixture, whisking until a thick, smooth batter forms. It should be thick enough to coat the chicken without dripping off too quickly.

instructions.title: Coat and Fry the Chicken (First Fry)
instructions.text: Dip each seasoned chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it's fully coated. Heat the vegetable oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 350F (175C). Carefully add the chicken pieces in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove the chicken and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a medium saucepan, combine the gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, brown sugar, minced garlic, minced ginger, sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water. Whisk well to combine all ingredients.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Place the saucepan over medium-low heat. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally. Let it simmer for 3-5 minutes, or until it slightly thickens and becomes glossy. Remove from heat and set aside.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375F (190C). Return the partially cooked chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This second fry is crucial for achieving the signature Korean fried chicken crunch. Remove and drain on the wire rack.

instructions.title: Toss and Serve
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, crispy chicken into a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken. Using tongs, gently toss the chicken until each piece is thoroughly coated in the sauce. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve hot and enjoy your authentic Yangnyeom Chicken!

tips: For the crispiest chicken, always do a double-fry. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry at a higher temperature makes the skin incredibly crisp.
Adjust the spice level by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochujang. For a sweeter sauce, add a bit more honey or corn syrup.
Yangnyeom Chicken is best enjoyed immediately after tossing in the sauce to maintain its crispiness. Serve with a side of pickled radish or rice.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey to Korea right from your kitchen! Korean Fried Chicken, or KFC as it's affectionately known, has taken the world by storm, captivating food lovers with its unparalleled crispiness and explosion of flavors. Among the many delectable styles, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken stands out as a true superstar. This iconic dish features incredibly crunchy fried chicken pieces coated in a glossy, irresistible sauce that perfectly balances sweet, spicy, and tangy notes.

If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean restaurant experience at home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master homemade Yangnyeom Chicken, from selecting the best ingredients to perfecting that signature double-fry technique and crafting the ultimate sauce. Prepare to impress your taste buds and your guests with one of the most beloved Korean Chicken Recipes!

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken translates literally to "seasoned chicken," and it’s a name that perfectly encapsulates its essence. Unlike its unadorned crispy counterpart, Yangnyeom Chicken is defined by its vibrant, sticky, and utterly addictive sauce that cloaks each piece of golden-fried poultry. The sauce is a complex symphony of flavors, typically featuring gochujang (Korean chili paste) for its distinctive heat and umami, balanced by sweetness from honey or corn syrup, tanginess from vinegar, and aromatic depth from garlic, ginger, and soy sauce.

The magic of Yangnyeom Chicken isn't just in the sauce; it’s also in the chicken itself. Korean fried chicken is famous for its almost glass-like thin, crisp crust, achieved through a unique double-frying method. This technique ensures the chicken remains incredibly moist on the inside while developing a shattering crunch on the outside that holds up remarkably well, even after being coated in the luscious sauce. This combination of superior texture and explosive flavor is what makes Yangnyeom Chicken a global sensation and a must-try for any fried chicken enthusiast.

## Ingredients You Need

To create authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, you’ll need a few key ingredients, some of which are staples in Korean pantries but are increasingly available in international supermarkets.

**For the Chicken:**
*   2 lbs (approx. 1 kg) chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs (cut into bite-sized pieces)
*   1 tablespoon soy sauce
*   1 teaspoon grated fresh ginger
*   2 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 cup potato starch (or cornstarch for a slightly less crispy texture)
*   1/2 cup all-purpose flour
*   1 teaspoon baking powder (optional, for extra crispiness)
*   Vegetable oil, for deep frying

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   1/4 cup gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   2 tablespoons honey or corn syrup
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 tablespoon minced garlic
*   1 teaspoon grated fresh ginger
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 tablespoon ketchup (optional, adds sweetness and tang)
*   2-3 tablespoons water or chicken broth (to adjust consistency)

**For Garnish:**
*   1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds
*   2 tablespoons chopped scallions

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve perfectly crispy and flavorful Yangnyeom Chicken.

**1. Prepare the Chicken:**
*   Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispiness.
*   In a large bowl, combine the chicken with soy sauce, grated ginger, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to coat and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.

**2. Prepare the Dredging Mixture:**
*   In a separate shallow bowl, whisk together the potato starch, all-purpose flour, and baking powder (if using).

**3. Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   In a small saucepan, combine all the sauce ingredients: gochujang, soy sauce, honey/corn syrup, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, ketchup (if using), and water/broth.
*   Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens slightly and becomes glossy (about 3-5 minutes). Remove from heat and set aside.

**4. First Fry:**
*   Heat about 3-4 inches of vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven to 350°F (175°C).
*   While the oil heats, dredge each marinated chicken piece thoroughly in the starch-flour mixture, ensuring it's fully coated. Shake off any excess.
*   Carefully lower the chicken pieces into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and lightly golden.
*   Remove the chicken from the oil with a slotted spoon or spider and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let it rest for 5-10 minutes.

**5. Second Fry:**
*   Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). This second fry is key for the signature Korean crispiness.
*   Return the rested chicken to the hotter oil, again frying in batches. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy.
*   Remove from oil and place on a clean wire rack to drain any excess oil.

**6. Coat and Serve:**
*   Once all the chicken is double-fried, transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl.
*   Pour the warmed Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken. Toss gently but quickly to ensure each piece is evenly coated.
*   Transfer the coated Yangnyeom Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped scallions. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Yangnyeom Chicken involves a few insider tricks that can elevate your homemade version to restaurant quality.

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret to the incredibly crisp crust that defines Korean Fried Chicken. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the resting period allows moisture to escape. The second, hotter fry creates that shatteringly crisp exterior. Don't skip it!
*   **Pat Your Chicken Dry:** Excess moisture on the chicken skin will prevent it from getting truly crispy. Always pat the chicken thoroughly dry before marinating and dredging.
*   **Use Potato Starch:** While cornstarch works, potato starch generally yields a lighter, crispier, and more delicate crust, which is ideal for Yangnyeom Chicken.
*   **Maintain Oil Temperature:** Use a cooking thermometer to monitor your oil temperature. Too low, and the chicken will be greasy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** Frying in small batches is essential. Overcrowding lowers the oil temperature, leading to soggy, poorly cooked chicken.
*   **Coat Immediately Before Serving:** The sauce is best applied to hot, freshly fried chicken. This ensures maximum adhesion and prevents the chicken from becoming soggy.
*   **Adjust Sauce to Taste:** Feel free to tweak the Yangnyeom sauce ingredients. Add more gochujang for extra heat, more honey for sweetness, or a dash more vinegar for tang.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Chicken is fantastic on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Korean accompaniments.

*   **Pickled Radish (Mu Sam):** The refreshing, tangy crunch of pickled daikon radish is the quintessential pairing for Korean Fried Chicken. Its acidity cuts through the richness of the chicken and sauce.
*   **Steamed Rice:** A bowl of fluffy white rice is perfect for soaking up any extra sauce and balancing the intense flavors.
*   **Kimchi and Banchan:** Serve alongside an assortment of kimchi and other Korean side dishes (banchan) for a complete Korean meal experience.
*   **Beer (Chimaek):** In Korea, fried chicken and beer ("Chimaek," a portmanteau of "chicken" and "maekju" meaning beer) is a beloved combination. A cold lager or ale is the perfect beverage.
*   **Soju:** For those who enjoy a stronger drink, a shot of chilled soju is another popular pairing.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Chicken is hard to beat, there are ways to adapt the recipe to suit different tastes or dietary needs.

*   **Spice Level:** To increase the heat, add a pinch of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) to the sauce, or simply increase the amount of gochujang. For a milder version, reduce the gochujang and add a bit more ketchup or honey.
*   **Sweetness:** Adjust the amount of honey or corn syrup in the sauce based on your preference for sweetness.
*   **Chicken Cut:** While wings and drumsticks are traditional, boneless chicken thighs or even chicken breast cutlets can be used for easier eating. Just adjust frying times accordingly.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use tamari instead of regular soy sauce and ensure your starch and flour are certified gluten-free.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** This sauce can be adapted for plant-based "chicken." Try coating crispy fried tofu, cauliflower florets, or oyster mushrooms in the sauce for a delicious vegetarian alternative.
*   **Baked or Air-Fried:** While deep-frying yields the best texture, you can bake or air-fry the chicken for a lighter option. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) or air fryer to 375°F (190°C), spray the coated chicken with oil, and cook until golden and crispy, flipping halfway. The texture won't be identical to double-fried, but it will still be delicious with the Yangnyeom sauce.
*   **Other Korean Chicken Recipes:** Explore other sauce options like Ganjang (soy-garlic chicken) or Honey Butter chicken using the same double-frying technique for the ultimate crispy chicken base.

Making Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of Korea right to your table. With its unbelievably crispy texture and a sauce that's a perfect harmony of sweet, spicy, and savory, this dish is sure to become a new favorite. Don't be intimidated by the double-frying; follow these steps, embrace the process, and get ready to enjoy the best homemade Korean Fried Chicken you've ever had!
